result.stdout:
Description:
  wrapper of dotnet test command with the extra option to automatic rerun failed tests

Usage:
  test-rerun [<path>] [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Arguments:
  <path>  Path to a test project .dll file.

Options:
  --filter <filter>                                           Run tests that match the given expression.
  -s, --settings <settings>                                   The run settings file to use when running tests.
  -l, --logger <logger>                                       Specifies a logger for test results. [default: trx]
  -r, --results-directory <results-directory>                 The directory where the test results will be placed.
                                                              The specified directory will be created if it does not exist. [default: .]
  --rerunMaxAttempts <rerunMaxAttempts>                       Maximum # of attempts. [default: 3]
  --rerunMaxFailedTests <rerunMaxFailedTests>                 Maximum # of failed tests to rerun. If exceeded, tests will not be rerun. [default: -1]
  --loglevel <Debug|Error|Information|Verbose|Warning>        Log Level [default: Verbose]
  --no-build                                                  Do not build the project before testing. Implies --no-restore.
  --no-restore                                                Do not restore the project before building.
  -d, --delay <delay>                                         Delay between test runs in seconds.
  --blame                                                     Runs the tests in blame mode.
  -c, --configuration <configuration>                         Defines the build configuration.
  -f, --framework <framework>                                 Defines the target framework to run the tests.
  -v, --verbosity <Detailed|Diagnostic|Minimal|Normal|Quiet>  Sets the verbosity level of the command. Possible values: Quiet, Minimal, Normal, Detailed, Diagnostic
  --deleteReports                                             Delete the report files generated.
  --collect <collect>                                         Enables data collector for the test run.
  --mergeCoverageFormat <Cobertura|Coverage|Xml>              Output coverage format. Note: requires dotnet coverage tool to be installed.
  --inlineRunSettings <inlineRunSettings>                     Specifies the inline run settings.
  -e, --environment <environment>                             Sets the value of an environment variable.
  -?, -h, --help                                              Show help and usage information
  --version                                                   Show version information

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

Usage:
